capability_objective: The dispatch lead is reconciling two vehicle records before assigning tomorrow’s routes. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the vehicle named “Box Truck 18” at “Warehouse Fleet” and the vehicle named “Passenger Van 23” at “Programs Fleet”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use exactly two bullets in the requested order; include the first status in bullet one and both the second status and comparison in bullet two.
prompt:
The dispatch lead is reconciling two vehicle records before assigning tomorrow’s routes. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Use the unmodified harness’s genuine shell/process-execution tool to run the local `./fleetctl` executable for every fleet lookup, and discover its interface from the executable’s built-in help before accessing vehicle data. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected files, runtime database, execution journal, setup program, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the vehicle named “Box Truck 18” at “Warehouse Fleet” and the vehicle named “Passenger Van 23” at “Programs Fleet”. Your first vehicle-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./fleetctl` processes, with no other fleet operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin a retrieval until both searches have returned. Treat the branches independently: retrieve a branch only if its own search returned exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID, and use only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following assistant action must start both full-record retrievals concurrently as two separate direct `./fleetctl` processes and wait for both. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve it; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take statuses for resolved branches only from successful full-record retrievals, not from search summaries or backing files.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter fleet data, inspect or edit the execution journal, expose unrelated records, or invoke any fleet operation beyond interface discovery, the two required searches, and eligible full-record retrievals.

Write the result to `audit.md` in the sandbox root. It must contain exactly two Markdown bullets, in the requested order, with no heading, blank lines, or other text. Format the first bullet exactly as `- Box Truck 18: <status or unresolved>`. Format the second exactly as `- Passenger Van 23: <status or unresolved>; comparison: <comparison>`. For a resolved branch, preserve the retrieved status exactly; for an unresolved branch, use the lowercase word `unresolved`. The comparison must be exactly `same as Box Truck 18` when both retrieved status strings match, `different from Box Truck 18` when both are retrieved and differ, or `unresolved` when either branch is unresolved. End both bullets with a newline. Run the protected verifier when finished, leave all supplied files unchanged, and make the final assistant response exactly the content of `audit.md`.
